reasoning: The document contains a critical programmatic defect due to pervasive header/footer retention across all pages, which will negatively impact screen readers. Additionally, a page break transition caused a complete omission of sentences (a major fidelity defect), and list structures were flattened. The presence of a critical defect mandates a REJECT routing.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (4): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 4; Page 3 of 4
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=4, pages_affected=4/4. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: Due to a page break transition issue from page 2 to page 3, a couple of sentences were completely dropped. This omission removes contextual information and a reference to an attachment, which alters the meaning/information provided to the reader.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: speak was finished. The list of speakers is included as Attachment II. The following is a summary of oral comments received at the public meeting summarized by topic (in no particular order):
concerns.output_quote: Four individuals registered before the meeting to make comments. Speakers were given five minutes for initial presentations and additional time was provided after everyone that registered to [TEXT ABRUPTLY ENDS] ... Environmental Justice – One speaker was from...
concerns.location: Transition between Page 2 and Page 3 (End of Section II)

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p1:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

The source document heavily uses bolding and underlining to emphasize key terms at the start of bullet points (e.g., 'Environmental Justice', 'Risk Classification', 'Costs', 'Excavation'). The extraction completely loses both the bolding (no markdown asterisks) and the underlining for these terms, rendering them as plain text. The underline is also lost on the 'MEMORANDUM' title.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Environmental Justice (Underlined and Bolded in PDF) – One speaker was from Lee County...
concerns.output_quote: "text": "Environmental Justice – One speaker was from Lee County..."
concerns.location: Page 3, Section II and Section III bullet points

action_items: Remove retained header and footer artifacts
Restore the dropped sentences from the transition between pages 2 and 3
Fix flattened list hierarchies to properly nest sub-points
Restore bold and underline emphasis on key terms
